FPC (Flexible Printed Circuit) Design Guideline

Introduction:

The purpose of this document is to provide a comprehensive guideline for designing FPCs (Flexible Printed Circuits) for use in Apple products. This document outlines the general requirements, material selection, and design considerations for FPCs.

Material Selection:

The following materials are recommended for FPCs:

* Copper foil (1 oz, 0.5 oz, or 1.5 oz)
* Polyimide film (25 um, 50 um, or 75 um)
* Adhesive (Acrylic or Epoxy)
* Solder mask (Green or Blue)
* Legend/Marking ink (Black or White)

Design Considerations:

* Minimum line width and spacing: 0.15 mm
* Minimum pad diameter: 0.25 mm
* Minimum via diameter: 0.15 mm
* Minimum annular ring: 0.075 mm
* Minimum solder mask web: 0.075 mm

The following question is based on the provided context:

What is the minimum line width and spacing for FPC design according to the provided guideline?

detailed_results.question: What is FPC design guideline of De-cap ?
detailed_results.expected_answer: When a flex needs to have different numbers of copper layers for different regions of the same flex, de-cap isrequired at the layer transition to cut away the excess layers. For example, a flex may have two layers of copper forhalf and be only one layer for the other half, with the layer transition being the area for de-cap. The cut awayprocess is done with either a pre-punch of PI and bonding sheet (usually only for 3+ layer flexes) before laminationor with a laser after lamination

FPC (Flexible Printed Circuit) Design Guideline

Introduction:

The purpose of this document is to provide a comprehensive guideline for designing FPCs (Flexible Printed Circuits) for use in Apple products. This guideline outlines the requirements and recommendations for designing FPCs to ensure compatibility, reliability, and performance in Apple products.

FPC Design Requirements:

1. Material Selection:

* The FPC material shall be selected from the following options: 
  - Polyimide (PI) 
  - Polyethylene terephthalate (PET) 
  - Polyethylene naphthalate (PEN) 
  - LCP (Liquid Crystal Polymer) 
  - Other materials as approved by Apple Inc.

2. Trace Width and Spacing:

* The minimum trace width shall be 0.15 mm 
  - The minimum trace spacing shall be 0.15 mm

3. Via Hole Size:

* The via hole size shall be 0.2 mm or larger

4. Solder Mask:

* The solder mask material shall be applied to all areas of the FPC except for the areas where components will be mounted.

5. Legend:

* The legend shall be placed on the component side of the FPC.

6. Impedance Control:

* The FPC design shall be capable of controlling impedance to ensure signal integrity.

7. Bending and Folding:

* The FPC shall be designed to withstand bending and folding without damage.

8. Environmental Testing:

* The FPC shall be designed to withstand environmental testing as specified in the Apple Environmental Testing Specification.

9. Regulatory Compliance:

* The FPC design shall comply with all applicable regulatory requirements.

10. Manufacturing Process:

* The FPC design shall be compatible with the manufacturing process as specified in the Apple Manufacturing Process Specification.

FPC (Flexible Printed Circuit) Design Guideline

Introduction:

The purpose of this document is to provide a comprehensive guideline for designing FPCs (Flexible Printed Circuits) for use in Apple products. The guidelines outlined in this document are intended to ensure the quality, reliability, and performance of FPCs used in Apple products.

Design Requirements:

The following are the general design requirements for FPCs:

1. Material: The FPC shall be made of a flexible material that can withstand the environmental conditions and mechanical stress encountered during the product lifecycle.

2. Thickness: The FPC thickness shall be 0.15mm ±0.03mm.

3. Copper Foil: The copper foil thickness shall be 0.035mm ±0.01mm.

4. Solder Mask: A solder mask layer shall be applied to the FPC to prevent solder bridging and to protect the copper foil from corrosion.

5. Legend: A legend layer shall be applied to the FPC to provide identification and instruction for assembly and repair.

6. Impedance: The FPC shall be designed to meet the impedance requirements as specified in the Apple impedance specification document.

7. Signal Integrity: The FPC shall be designed to meet the signal integrity requirements as specified in the Apple signal integrity specification document.

8. Bending Radius: The FPC shall be designed to withstand a minimum bending radius of 1mm.

9. Tear Resistance: The FPC shall be designed to withstand a minimum tear resistance of 10N.

10. Adhesion: The FPC shall be designed to meet the adhesion requirements as specified in the Apple adhesion specification document.

11. Environmental: The FPC shall be designed to meet the environmental requirements as specified in the Apple environmental specification document.

12. Marking: The FPC shall have the Apple logo, part number, and serial number marked on it.

13. Packaging: The FPC shall be packaged in accordance with the Apple packaging specification document.

FPC (Flexible Printed Circuit) Design Guideline

Introduction:

The purpose of this document is to provide a comprehensive guideline for designing FPCs (Flexible Printed Circuits) for use in Apple products. The guidelines outlined in this document are intended to ensure the quality, reliability, and performance of FPCs used in Apple devices.

FPC Design Requirements:

The following are the general design requirements for FPCs:

1. Material Selection:

* The FPC material shall be selected based on the application requirements, taking into consideration factors such as flexibility, temperature range, and chemical resistance.

* The FPC material shall meet the following specifications:

+ UL 94 V-0 rating or equivalent

+ Tg > 150°C

* The adhesive used for bonding the FPC to other components shall meet the following specifications:

+ Adhesive thickness: 25-50 μm

+ Adhesive strength: ≥ 10 N/cm²

2. Trace Design:

* The trace width and spacing shall be designed to meet the signal integrity requirements of the application.

* The trace width and spacing shall be as follows:

+ Minimum trace width: 50 μm

+ Minimum trace spacing: 50 μm

* The trace corners shall be rounded with a radius of ≥ 25 μm to reduce stress concentrations.

3. Via Design:

* The via diameter shall be ≥ 100 μm.

* The via annular ring shall be ≥ 50 μm.

* The via plating thickness shall be ≥ 20 μm.

4. Solder Mask and Legend:

* The solder mask material shall meet the following specifications:

+ UL 94 V-0 rating or equivalent

+ Tg > 150°C

* The solder mask thickness shall be 20-30 μm.

* The legend material shall meet the following specifications:

+ Color: White

+ Font size: ≥ 1.5 mm

5. FPC Thickness:

* The FPC thickness shall be 0.15-0.30 mm.

6. FPC Bending:

* The FPC shall be designed to withstand a minimum of 1000 bending cycles without damage.

* The FPC bending radius shall be ≥ 1 mm.

7. Electrical Performance:

* The FPC shall meet the following electrical performance requirements:

+ Impedance: 50 Ω ± 10%

+ Capacitance: ≤ 10 pF

+ Inductance: ≤ 10 nH

8. Environmental Requirements:

* The FPC shall meet the following environmental requirements:

+ Operating temperature: -20°C to 85°C

+ Storage temperature: -40°C to 85°C

+ Humidity: ≤ 60%

9. Regulatory Requirements:

* The FPC shall comply with the following regulatory requirements:

+ RoHS

+ REACH

+ WEEE

10. Inspection and Testing:

* The FPC shall be inspected and tested according to the following procedures:

+ Visual inspection

+ Electrical testing

+ Environmental testing

detailed_results.question: What is FPC reflow test acceptance criteria?
detailed_results.expected_answer: function, SUS stiffener resistance, cosmetics, BVH peel fir multi-layer FPC, cross-section
detailed_results.actual_answer:
The FPC reflow test acceptance criteria is not explicitly stated in the provided context documents. However, related information can be found in the documents.

According to Page 21 of document 080-02647-J_PCB_FPC_Line_Qualification.pdf, the reflow test is mentioned as one of the CQRA test items for FPC, but the acceptance criteria are not specified.

Additionally, Page 22 of the same document mentions reflow as a test item, but again, the acceptance criteria are not provided.

It is also mentioned on Page 25 of document 080-02647-J_PCB_FPC_Line_Qualification.pdf that reflow is a test item for flash Cu plating, but the acceptance criteria are not specified.

Since the acceptance criteria for the FPC reflow test are not found in the provided context documents, it cannot be determined from the given information.

- 080-02647-J_PCB_FPC_Line_Qualification.pdf (Pages 21, 22, 25)

detailed_results.question: What is CQRA of FPC line qualification.
detailed_results.expected_answer:
Thermal cycle
• Hot Oil
• Reflow
• Insulation resistance (via to via)
• Thermal stability (Solder shock) fo
detailed_results.actual_answer:
The CQRA (Critical Quality and Reliability Attributes) of FPC line qualification includes various test items, which are specified in different sections of the document 080-02647-J_PCB_FPC_Line_Qualification.pdf.

For FPC, the CQRA test items are mentioned in the following sections:
- Section 8.1.3 (Page 18): Thermal cycle, Hot Oil, Reflow, Insulation resistance (via to via), and Thermal stability (thermal stress for FPC) [1].
- Section 8.2.5 (Page 27): Insulation resistance (trace to trace) [2].
- Section 8.3.7 (Page 43): Reflow, Heat soak, Insulation resistance (trace to trace), and Thermal stability (thermal stress for FPC) [3].
- Section 8.3.8 (Page 44): Reflow, Heat soak, Insulation resistance (layer to layer), and Thermal stability (thermal stress for FPC) [4].
- Section 8.6.5.2 (Page 63): Solder-ability test, Ionic contamination, and High speed ball shear test [5].

The test methods and sampling plan for these CQRA test items are referred to other documents: 099-01674 for rigid and rigid-flex PCB, and CQRA 080-02999 and QCP 080-03022 (NPI level frequency) for FPC.

References:
[1] 080-02647-J_PCB_FPC_Line_Qualification.pdf, Page 18
[2] 080-02647-J_PCB_FPC_Line_Qualification.pdf, Page 27
[3] 080-02647-J_PCB_FPC_Line_Qualification.pdf, Page 43
[4] 080-02647-J_PCB_FPC_Line_Qualification.pdf, Page 44
[5] 080-02647-J_PCB_FPC_Line_Qualification.pdf, Page 63
